title: Robin
description: A Robin that comes to my bird feeder every single day. Robins are famously bold and curious, and this one is no exception — always the first to arrive and the last to leave.

title: Maple the Fox
description: This is Maple, a fox who visits the garden once every two or three days. She seems comfortable around people and always has an air of quiet confidence about her.

title: Maple Resting
description: Maple making herself at home in the backyard. She sometimes just settles down and waits, clearly knowing she'll be fed eventually. Urban foxes are remarkably good at training their humans.

title: Redwing
description: A Redwing caught mid-meal on a berry. Redwings are winter visitors to the UK, arriving from Scandinavia and Iceland. They occasionally drop by the yard, and it's always a treat when they do.

title: Chiffchaff
description: A Chiffchaff spotted at the Walthamstow Wetlands. These small warblers are one of the first migrants to arrive in spring, and you often hear their repetitive "chiff-chaff" call before you spot them.

title: Long-tailed Tit
description: A Long-tailed Tit at the Walthamstow Wetlands. These tiny, fluffy birds travel in noisy flocks and are almost impossibly cute up close. Getting a sharp shot of one sitting still for even a moment is a small victory.

title: Swan at Walthamstow Wetlands
description: A swan gliding through the Walthamstow Wetlands. The wetlands are a hidden gem in the middle of London — a vast nature reserve that feels miles away from the city surrounding it.

title: Willow the Fox
description: This is Willow, a fox who visits the garden occasionally. She is currently pregnant, which is wonderful — but she is also suffering from mange. I am hoping to earn her trust so I can slip medication into her food.

title: Willow's Breakfast
description: Willow tucking into an egg I left out for her. The plan is to get her visiting regularly and comfortable enough to eat here, so I can start hiding mange medication in her food.

title: Willow
description: Another visit from Willow. Despite her condition, she carries herself with dignity. Fingers crossed the treatment works and she gets to raise her cubs healthy.
